mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the housing market index (HOUST) over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in consumer confidence and demand for durable goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if HOUST does not exceed 2 standard deviations over the next 5 trading days, or if 20-day forward returns in periods with a 2-standard-deviation HOUST rise are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth and increases corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if UNRATE does not decline below 4.5% over the next 3 months, or if 60-day forward returns in periods with a UNRATE below 4.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the industrial production index (INDPRO) over the next 20 trading days signals a surge in supply chain capacity and productivity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if INDPRO does not exceed 2 standard deviations over the next 20 trading days, or if 20-day forward returns in periods with a 2-standard-deviation INDPRO rise are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in real disposable personal income (GDPC1) above 2% signals a surge in consumer spending and demand for durable goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if GDPC1 does not rise above 2% over the next 3 months, or if 60-day forward returns in periods with a GDPC1 rise above 2% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) over the next 5 trading days signals a surge in inflation expectations and interest rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if CPIAUCSL does not exceed 2 standard deviations over the next 5 trading days, or if 5-day forward returns in periods with a 2-standard-deviation CPIAUCSL rise are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the employment rate (PAYEMS) above 150,000 signals a surge in labor market growth and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if PAYEMS does not rise above 150,000 over the next 3 months, or if 20-day forward returns in periods with a PAYEMS rise above 150,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-standard-deviation rise in the real sales of retail trade (RSAFS) over the next 60 trading days signals a surge in consumer spending and demand for durable goods, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if RSAFS does not exceed 2 standard deviations over the next 60 trading days, or if 60-day forward returns in periods with a 2-standard-deviation RSAFS rise are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
